title: Data-Driven EC2 Rightsizing
description:
## Recommendations to Downsize Over-Provisioned EC2

An organization suspects many of its EC2 instances are over-provisioned. They want **specific, data-driven recommendations** — derived from actual CPU, memory, network, and disk utilization metrics — telling them which instances to downsize or move to a different/cheaper instance type, ideally with projected savings and performance-risk ratings. They want this for EC2 instances, Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, and Lambda functions, with minimal manual analysis. Which AWS service BEST provides this?

options.text: AWS Cost Explorer rightsizing report only

explanation: **AWS Compute Optimizer** analyzes historical utilization metrics with machine learning and produces granular rightsizing recommendations for **EC2 instances, Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, and Lambda functions**, including the recommended instance type, projected savings, and a performance-risk assessment — exactly what is described. **Trusted Advisor** (B) includes a cost category that flags idle/underutilized resources, but its recommendations are coarser best-practice checks, not ML-driven rightsizing across all those resource types. **CloudWatch** (C) supplies the raw metrics but does no analysis or recommendation — you'd interpret it manually. **Cost Explorer** (D) offers an EC2 rightsizing recommendation feature but it is narrower (EC2-focused) and the question asks for ASG/EBS/Lambda coverage with utilization-based ML recommendations, which is Compute Optimizer's remit.

hints.title: Who turns metrics into recommendations?
hints.body: CloudWatch gives metrics; the requirement is for a service that converts utilization into concrete downsizing recommendations.

hints.title: Breadth of resources
hints.body: The scope (EC2, ASG, EBS, Lambda) points to the dedicated ML rightsizing service rather than the broad best-practice checker.
